rule.statement: All four tools MUST consume the same OTEL span tree; never emit a fork-per-tool.
rule.rationale: Fork-per-tool doubles network spend and breaks correlation across UIs.

rule.statement: PII redaction MUST happen at the SDK wrapper edge, BEFORE the OTEL exporter ships.
rule.rationale: Redacting after export defeats the point and is GDPR-non-compliant.

rule.statement: Self-hosted Langfuse + Phoenix MUST be chosen when EU data-residency is required.
rule.rationale: Closed-cloud observability cannot satisfy data-residency for EU customers.

rule.statement: Helicone MUST be the canonical source-of-truth for cost-by-feature; never re-derive from raw spans.
rule.rationale: Multiple cost sources drift; pick one canonical.

rule.statement: Arize Phoenix MUST be the canonical eval dashboard surface.
rule.rationale: Multiple eval surfaces fragment stakeholder attention.

rule.statement: Span emission MUST be budget-capped (e.g. 10MB/min) with sampling fallback.
rule.rationale: Unbounded span emission causes pipeline back-pressure.
